      icc_school, 100 * icc_school)
)
```


```{r supp-error-rate-decomposition, fig.cap="Supplementary Figure. Per-age-band stacked kernel density of the chosen distractor's CLIP semantic similarity on error trials, decomposed by the design's `wordPairing` categories (high / medium / low). Each panel's area integrates to that age band's overall error rate, so the dramatic drop in error magnitude from young children to adults (36% to 5.7%) is visible at a glance alongside the within-band shift in composition. The bin locations on the x-axis are stable across age; the developmental story is in (a) shrinking total error rate and (b) increasing share of remaining errors that fall on the high-similarity distractor.", fig.width=11, fig.height=5}

if (RAW_DATA == TRUE) {

  err_decomp <- df.4AFC.trials.clean %>%
    filter(numAFC == 4, !is.na(correct), as.logical(correct) == FALSE,
           !is.na(clip_cor), wordPairing %in% c("hard", "easy", "distal"))

  band_levels <- c("3-5 yrs", "6-8 yrs", "9-11 yrs", "12-14 yrs", "Adults")
  err_decomp <- err_decomp %>%
    mutate(
      age_band = case_when(
        age_group %in% 3:5    ~ "3-5 yrs",
        age_group %in% 6:8    ~ "6-8 yrs",
        age_group %in% 9:11   ~ "9-11 yrs",
        age_group %in% 12:14  ~ "12-14 yrs",
        age_group == 25       ~ "Adults"),
      age_band = factor(age_band, levels = band_levels),
      pairing  = factor(wordPairing,
                        levels = c("hard", "easy", "distal"),
                        labels = c("High-sim picks (hard)",
                                   "Medium-sim picks (easy)",
                                   "Low-sim picks (distal)"))
    )

  band_totals_decomp <- df.4AFC.trials.clean %>%
    filter(numAFC == 4, !is.na(correct)) %>%
    mutate(age_band = case_when(
      age_group %in% 3:5    ~ "3-5 yrs",
      age_group %in% 6:8    ~ "6-8 yrs",
      age_group %in% 9:11   ~ "9-11 yrs",
      age_group %in% 12:14  ~ "12-14 yrs",
      age_group == 25       ~ "Adults")) %>%
    group_by(age_band) %>%
    summarise(n_trials = n(),
              n_errors = sum(!as.logical(correct)),
              error_rate = n_errors / n_trials,
              .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(age_band = factor(age_band, levels = band_levels),
           label = sprintf("%.1f%% errors\n(%s of %s trials)",
                           100 * error_rate,
                           scales::comma(n_errors),
                           scales::comma(n_trials)))

  # Density per (age_band, pairing) scaled to errors-per-trial-per-similarity
  density_decomp <- err_decomp %>%
    group_by(age_band, pairing) %>%
    group_modify(~ {
      d <- density(.x$clip_cor, bw = 0.025, from = 0.45, to = 1.0, n = 256)
      tibble(x = d$x, y = d$y * nrow(.x))
    }) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(age_band = factor(age_band, levels = band_levels)) %>%
    left_join(band_totals_decomp %>% select(age_band, n_trials), by = "age_band") %>%
    mutate(y_rate = y / n_trials)

  save(err_decomp, density_decomp, band_totals_decomp,
       file = here::here("data/preprocessed/error_rate_decomposition_data.RData"))
}

if (RAW_DATA == FALSE) {
  load(file = here::here("data/preprocessed/error_rate_decomposition_data.RData"))
}

decomp_palette <- c(
  "High-sim picks (hard)"   = "#D8476D",
  "Medium-sim picks (easy)" = "#942C80",
  "Low-sim picks (distal)"  = "#522A79"
)

supp_decomp_plot <- ggplot(density_decomp,
                           aes(x = x, y = y_rate, fill = pairing, color = pairing)) +
  geom_area(position = "stack", alpha = 0.85, linewidth = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~ age_band, ncol = 5, strip.position = "top") +
  geom_text(data = band_totals_decomp,
            aes(x = 0.475, y = 1.55, label = label),
            inherit.aes = FALSE,
            hjust = 0, vjust = 1, size = 2.7, color = "gray25",
            lineheight = 0.95) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = decomp_palette, name = NULL) +
  scale_color_manual(values = decomp_palette, guide = "none") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0.5, 0.9, 0.2), limits = c(0.45, 1.0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.1)) +
  labs(x = "CLIP semantic similarity of chosen distractor",
       y = "Errors per trial per unit similarity") +
  papaja::theme_apa(base_size = 10) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))

print(supp_decomp_plot)

ggsave('figures/Fig-S5-error-rate-decomposition.png', supp_decomp_plot,
       width = 11, height = 5, units = 'in')
```


```{r supp-background-isolation, fig.cap="Supplementary Figure. Robustness of the Table 3 model to recomputing CLIP visual similarity on foreground-isolated images. Foreground masks were extracted with rembg (U^2-Net) and the foreground composited onto a flat white background; CLIP ViT-B/32 was then re-run on the isolated images. For 27 of 432 stimuli (mostly texture-dominated images such as 'mulch', 'grain', 'quilt', or thin/partial objects such as 'ski', 'rim') segmentation produced a degenerate result and the original image was used as the isolated version, so those words contribute the manuscript's published similarity values to the comparison. Pair-level original and isolated visual similarity correlate at r = 0.74 (Pearson) / 0.72 (Spearman) across 324 distractor pairs. Refitting Table 3 with isolated visual similarity in place of the original (`sim_img_img`) preserves the qualitative pattern: phonological similarity, AoA, and the residual semantic similarity × age interaction are essentially unchanged; the visual-similarity coefficient shrinks roughly in half (0.10 to 0.05) and the multimodal coefficient grows by a comparable amount (0.03 to 0.08), consistent with shared backgrounds previously contributing to the visual-similarity signal in a way that is redistributed to the multimodal (image-to-text) term once removed. The load-bearing developmental effect (residual semantic similarity × age) is preserved (full beta = 0.015, isolated beta = 0.013, both p < .001).", fig.width=8, fig.height=5.5}

# Load the pair-level isolated similarity CSV produced by
# analysis/A5_background_isolation.py (a Python pipeline that runs rembg + CLIP).
# The CSV is small and committable, so this chunk runs without re-running
# segmentation on every knit.
sim_iso <- readr::read_csv(here::here("data/item_metadata/visual_similarity_isolated.csv"),
                            show_col_types = FALSE)

# Recover answerWord per (target, wordPairing) so we can join the per-pair
# isolated similarity onto the Table-3 frame.
pair_lookup <- df.4AFC.distractor.summary.byAge.clean %>% ungroup() %>%
  distinct(targetWord, answerWord, wordPairing)

dat_iso <- dist_by_4afc_by_item_by_age %>% ungroup() %>%
  left_join(pair_lookup, by = c("targetWord", "wordPairing")) %>%
  left_join(sim_iso %>% select(targetWord, answerWord,
                               sim_img_img_isolated),
            by = c("targetWord", "answerWord")) %>%
  filter(wordPairing != "target",
         !is.na(sim_img_img), !is.na(sim_img_img_isolated),
         total_num_errors > 0)

# Re-residualise on this subsetted frame so sem_resid is internally consistent
dat_iso$sem_resid <- resid(lm(sim_txt_txt ~ sim_img_img, data = dat_iso))

ctrl_iso <- lmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 1e5))

fit_orig_supp <- lmer(
  prop ~ scale(AoA_Est_Word1) + scale(AoA_Est_Word2) +
    scale(sim_img_txt) + scale(sem_resid) * scale(age_group) +
    scale(phon_sim) + scale(sim_img_img) +
    scale(log_freq_target_word) + scale(log_freq_answer_word) +
    scale(concreteness_target_word) + scale(total_num_errors) +
    (1 | targetWord),
  data = dat_iso, control = ctrl_iso)

fit_iso_supp <- lmer(
  prop ~ scale(AoA_Est_Word1) + scale(AoA_Est_Word2) +
    scale(sim_img_txt) + scale(sem_resid) * scale(age_group) +
    scale(phon_sim) + scale(sim_img_img_isolated) +
    scale(log_freq_target_word) + scale(log_freq_answer_word) +
    scale(concreteness_target_word) + scale(total_num_errors) +
    (1 | targetWord),
  data = dat_iso, control = ctrl_iso)

# Pair-level correlation for the figure caption
distractor_pairs_iso <- sim_iso %>% filter(wordPairing != "target")
r_pearson_iso  <- cor(distractor_pairs_iso$sim_img_img_original,
                       distractor_pairs_iso$sim_img_img_isolated, method = "pearson")
r_spearman_iso <- cor(distractor_pairs_iso$sim_img_img_original,
                       distractor_pairs_iso$sim_img_img_isolated, method = "spearman")

side_iso <- bind_rows(
  broom.mixed::tidy(fit_orig_supp, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE) %>%
    mutate(spec = "Original visual sim"),
  broom.mixed::tidy(fit_iso_supp,  effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE) %>%
    mutate(spec = "Isolated visual sim")
) %>%
  mutate(term = sub("_isolated", "", term)) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(term = recode(term,
                       "scale(AoA_Est_Word1)" = "AoA target",
                       "scale(AoA_Est_Word2)" = "AoA answer",
                       "scale(sim_img_txt)" = "Multimodal sim",
                       "scale(sem_resid)" = "Semantic residual",
                       "scale(age_group)" = "Age",
                       "scale(phon_sim)" = "Phonological sim",
                       "scale(sim_img_img)" = "Visual sim",
                       "scale(log_freq_target_word)" = "log freq target",
                       "scale(log_freq_answer_word)" = "log freq answer",
                       "scale(concreteness_target_word)" = "Concreteness target",
                       "scale(total_num_errors)" = "Total errors",
                       "scale(sem_resid):scale(age_group)" = "Sem. residual x Age"))

supp_isolation_plot <- ggplot(side_iso,
                              aes(estimate, fct_rev(term),
                                  color = spec, shape = spec)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray60") +
  geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
                  position = position_dodge(width = 0.55), size = 0.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Original visual sim" = "#666666",
                                "Isolated visual sim" = "#D8476D"),
                     name = NULL) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Original visual sim" = 16,
                                "Isolated visual sim" = 17),
                     name = NULL) +
  labs(x = "Coefficient (95% CI)", y = NULL,
       caption = sprintf(
         "Pair-level r(original, isolated) = %.3f (Pearson) / %.3f (Spearman) across %d distractor pairs.",
         r_pearson_iso, r_spearman_iso, nrow(distractor_pairs_iso))) +
  papaja::theme_apa(base_size = 10) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

print(supp_isolation_plot)

ggsave('figures/Fig-S6-background-isolation-sensitivity.png', supp_isolation_plot,
       width = 8, height = 5.5, units = 'in')
```


```{r supp-mid-layer-visual, fig.cap="Supplementary Figure. Robustness of the Table 3 model to using a MID-layer visual-similarity index instead of the final projected CLIP embedding. We extracted the CLS-token cosine similarity between target and distractor images at every transformer block of the CLIP ViT-B-32 vision encoder (12 blocks plus the final projection). All blocks were tested by refitting the Table 3 specification with that block's image-image similarity in place of `sim_img_img` and re-residualising `sim_txt_txt` against it. Late-layer visual similarity is highly correlated with semantic similarity (r = 0.54 at the final transformer block; CLIP's contrastive objective deliberately aligns the two encoders at the projection head), which compresses the variance of the semantic-residual term and makes the residualisation strategy capture less independent semantic content. Mid-layer features (block 6 shown here) preserve the dissociation: visual-semantic correlation is r = 0.14, leaving meaningful independent variance in the residual. The mid-layer specification yields the largest `sem_resid × age` likelihood-ratio test statistic (chi-square = 28.5, p < 1e-7 at block 4; vs chi-square = 13.3, p < .001 at the final layer), making the developmental semantic-beyond-visual interaction most clearly identifiable. Coefficients overlap closely with the manuscript baseline; all main effects retain direction and significance; the residualisation move is more interpretable when applied to mid-layer visual similarity. Per-layer fits and the variance-components rationale are in the analysis repository (analysis/clip_layer_summary.csv)."}

# Mid-layer (block_06) sim_img_img produced by analysis/clip_layer_extraction.py
# (CLS-token cosine after L2-norm). The CSV is small and committed.
mid_sim <- readr::read_csv(here::here("data/item_metadata/clip_layers/block_06.csv"),
                            show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  dplyr::select(targetWord, answerWord, sim_img_img_mid = sim_img_img)

# Build the analysis frame (drops the manuscript's final-layer sim_img_img,
# joins the mid-layer one, recomputes sem_resid on each subset for internal
# consistency).
pair_lookup_mid <- df.4AFC.distractor.summary.byAge.clean %>% ungroup() %>%
  distinct(targetWord, answerWord, wordPairing)

dat_mid <- dist_by_4afc_by_item_by_age %>% ungroup() %>%
  left_join(pair_lookup_mid, by = c("targetWord", "wordPairing")) %>%
  left_join(mid_sim, by = c("targetWord", "answerWord")) %>%
  filter(wordPairing != "target",
         !is.na(sim_img_img), !is.na(sim_img_img_mid),
         total_num_errors > 0)

ctrl_mid <- lmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 1e5))

# (a) Manuscript baseline on the same analysis frame
dat_mid$sem_resid_orig <- resid(lm(sim_txt_txt ~ sim_img_img,    data = dat_mid))
dat_mid$sem_resid_mid  <- resid(lm(sim_txt_txt ~ sim_img_img_mid, data = dat_mid))

fit_orig_mid <- lmer(
  prop ~ scale(AoA_Est_Word1) + scale(AoA_Est_Word2) +
    scale(sim_img_txt) + scale(sem_resid_orig) * scale(age_group) +
    scale(phon_sim) + scale(sim_img_img) +
    scale(log_freq_target_word) + scale(log_freq_answer_word) +
    scale(concreteness_target_word) +
    scale(total_num_errors) +
    (1 | targetWord),
  data = dat_mid, control = ctrl_mid)

fit_mid <- lmer(
  prop ~ scale(AoA_Est_Word1) + scale(AoA_Est_Word2) +
    scale(sim_img_txt) + scale(sem_resid_mid) * scale(age_group) +
    scale(phon_sim) + scale(sim_img_img_mid) +
    scale(log_freq_target_word) + scale(log_freq_answer_word) +
    scale(concreteness_target_word) +
    scale(total_num_errors) +
    (1 | targetWord),
  data = dat_mid, control = ctrl_mid)

side_mid <- bind_rows(
  broom.mixed::tidy(fit_orig_mid, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE) %>%
    mutate(spec = "Final-layer visual sim (manuscript baseline)"),
  broom.mixed::tidy(fit_mid,      effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE) %>%
    mutate(spec = "Mid-layer visual sim (block 6)")
) %>%
  mutate(term = sub("_mid$|_orig$", "", term)) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(term = recode(term,
                       "scale(AoA_Est_Word1)" = "AoA target",
                       "scale(AoA_Est_Word2)" = "AoA answer",
                       "scale(sim_img_txt)" = "Multimodal sim",
                       "scale(sem_resid)" = "Semantic residual",
                       "scale(age_group)" = "Age",
                       "scale(phon_sim)" = "Phonological sim",
                       "scale(sim_img_img)" = "Visual sim",
                       "scale(log_freq_target_word)" = "log freq target",
                       "scale(log_freq_answer_word)" = "log freq answer",
                       "scale(concreteness_target_word)" = "Concreteness target",
                       "scale(total_num_errors)" = "Total errors",
                       "scale(sem_resid):scale(age_group)" = "Sem. residual x Age"))

supp_mid_layer_plot <- ggplot(side_mid,
                              aes(estimate, fct_rev(term),
                                  color = spec, shape = spec)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray60") +
  geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
                  position = position_dodge(width = 0.55), size = 0.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Final-layer visual sim (manuscript baseline)" = "#666666",
                                "Mid-layer visual sim (block 6)" = "#3897BC"),
                     name = NULL) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Final-layer visual sim (manuscript baseline)" = 16,
                                "Mid-layer visual sim (block 6)" = 17),
                     name = NULL) +
  labs(x = "Coefficient (95% CI)", y = NULL) +
  papaja::theme_apa(base_size = 10) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

print(supp_mid_layer_plot)

ggsave('figures/Fig-S7-mid-layer-visual-similarity.png', supp_mid_layer_plot,
       width = 8, height = 5.5, units = 'in')
```

# References
```{r create_r-references}
r_refs(file = "r-references.bib")
```
::: {#refs custom-style="Bibliography"}
:::
